title: Rivian Q3 2024 Earnings Preview: Key Metrics
text: Rivian delivered 10,018 vehicles in Q3 2024, representing a significant 27% drop from Q2's 13,790 units. Due to a supplier shortage affecting copper windings, the company slashed its annual production guidance from 57,000 to 47,000-49,000 vehicles. Analysts anticipated approximately $1 billion in quarterly revenue, marking a 26% decline from the $1.34 billion reported in Q3 2023. This would represent Rivian's first revenue contraction since going public. Despite lower output, Rivian's per-unit losses are expected to improve. The company is projected to report a $0.96 loss per share in Q3, compared to $1.19 in the prior year, thanks to cost-reduction initiatives and manufacturing upgrades at its Normal, Illinois facility. Rivian's stock has declined over 53% throughout 2024 and remains down approximately 90% from its November 2021 public debut peak. The R2 model, launching in early 2026 at $45,000, represents a major growth opportunity. The company also secured backing from Volkswagen, which committed up to $5 billion in investment for next-generation electric vehicle development.
